task_completion.description: Open the specified dwanko_run.tnz animation scene in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is visible, and the title bar, Xsheet, or timeline indicates that 'dwanko_run.tnz' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Apply a circular matte effect to the character animation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX Schematic or Xsheet shows a masking/matte setup (e.g., 'Matte In' node) connecting the character animation and a circular shape/mask level. The viewer should show the character constrained within a circle.

task_completion.description: Set the render output format to PNG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog in OpenToonz is open, with the file format explicitly set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Set the render output path to the specified matte_test directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog shows the 'Save In' or output path configured to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/matte_test/'.

task_completion.description: Render at least 24 frames of the sequence to the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window displays the contents of '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/matte_test/', showing at least 24 generated PNG files. Thumbnails (if visible) should show the character inside a circular mask with a transparent background.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to apply the effect and render the animation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with OpenToonz menus, schematic, and output settings rather than terminal commands using external tools like ImageMagick or FFmpeg to crop the images.

integrity.description: The frames were genuinely rendered from OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: A render progress bar or rendering window from OpenToonz is visible in the screenshots, proving the software generated the files.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or text editor to spoof the output directory or bypass the OpenToonz application.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no open text editors or terminal windows running scripts to artificially create the output directory and populate it with dummy files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is located at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The required output directory is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/matte_test'. The agent must render a minimum of 24 frames as PNG files. The verification checks that the output files exist and that there is motion (pixel differences) between the first and last frames.
